objective: Add cache garbage collection + size limit to bridge_helper.sh — eliminate monotonic cache growth from long-running bridge_health cron polling.

problem.description: Post omega-bridge-2: filesystem cache stores per-bridge response bodies at $CACHE_DIR/<bridge>_<sha1(url)>.body. TTL is honored on read (stale → bypass + refetch) but stale entries are NEVER deleted. URL drift, helper-call diversity, and cron cadence (~16 entries/run × N runs/day) cause unbounded directory growth.
problem.impact: Disk leak + filesystem clutter. Manual workaround was rm -rf $CACHE_DIR; helper now self-manages.

cache-gc.behaviour: Phase A age-based eviction (.body + matching .meta), Phase B size-limit oldest-first eviction.

BRIDGE_HELPER_CACHE_GC_AGE_S.purpose: Age threshold (seconds) for cache-gc deletion (default 2h = max(TTL×2, 1h) heuristic)

BRIDGE_HELPER_CACHE_MAX_BYTES.purpose: Max total cache dir size in bytes (default 50 MB). 0 disables size-limit phase.

BRIDGE_HELPER_GC_PROBABILITY.purpose: Probabilistic gc trigger frequency on fetch: 1-in-N (RANDOM % N == 0). 0 disables; 1 = every fetch (test mode).

probabilistic_trigger.implementation: _maybe_gc_trigger() invoked at top of cmd_fetch (after CACHE_DIR ensured, before cache-key derivation). Silent: stdout suppressed, only verbose stderr trace.
probabilistic_trigger.race_safety: Single-pass per-trigger; rm -f is no-op if file vanishes mid-pass. No double-delete because each cmd_cache_gc invocation runs sequentially within the calling fetch process.

size_limit_implemented.phase: Phase B of cache-gc, runs after age eviction.
size_limit_implemented.algorithm: Sum sizes of remaining .body files; if total > max_bytes, sort by mtime ascending (eldest first), delete .body+.meta until under limit.
size_limit_implemented.validation_result: 10 files × 1000 B with 4000 B limit → 4 files × 1000 B = 4000 B kept (oldest-first eviction confirmed by mtime stagger).

size_gc_test.setup: 10×1000 B fresh files, mtime staggered 1-10 minutes ago, AGE=86400 (no age eviction), MAX_BYTES=4000
size_gc_test.actual_summary: scanned=10 kept=4 deleted=6 bytes_freed=6000; 4×1000 B remain (most-recent mtimes)

fetch_cache_hit_test.setup: Pre-seeded cache body for invalid URL, fetch invoked
fetch_cache_hit_test.expected: Body returned from cache, no network call

probabilistic_trigger_test.setup: GC_PROBABILITY=1 forces gc every fetch; one stale junk file + one fresh-cache probe
probabilistic_trigger_test.expected: Stale junk deleted by gc-side-effect, fresh cache hit returns body

constraints_honored.hexa_only_session: helper is bash; no hexa source touched

rollback.method_b: Set BRIDGE_HELPER_GC_PROBABILITY=0 to neutralize the inline trigger; cache-gc subcommand becomes opt-in only. cmd_cache_clear and cmd_cache_stat unchanged — original cache lifecycle still available.
rollback.blast_radius: Self-contained: cache-gc adds new code paths; existing fetch / cache-clear / cache-stat unchanged in default behaviour. Probabilistic trigger fires ~1% of fetches and cannot delete fresh entries.

compliance_notes: raw 66 (reason+fix on diags retained), raw 71 (gc summary on stdout, verbose trace on stderr — body unchanged). No new attack surface; all writes confined to existing $CACHE_DIR.
